market_summary.market_breadth: Mixed. S&P and Nasdaq near record highs but Dow led decline. 1.1% Dow drop on geopolitical risk (Iran/UAE ceasefire tension) while tech held ground.
market_summary.advance_decline_line: NYSE McClellan Oscillator +59.22 (May 2), down from +65.48 (Apr 30). Still positive but moderating breadth thrust.
market_summary.new_highs_lows: S&P 500, Nasdaq Composite, Nasdaq 100, Russell 2000 all hit record closing highs on May 1. May 4 saw modest pullback from peaks.

fear_greed_index.assessment: Index holding in upper Greed zone. 5 of 7 components at Greed/Extreme Greed. Stock price strength alone in Fear, suggesting some internal dispersion despite headline index records.

vix.assessment: VIX popped +7.65% Monday on Iran/UAE geopolitical escalation. 18.29 is still historically low-moderate (long-term avg ~20). Premarket Tuesday shows slight easing to ~17.65. No panic priced in.
vix.term_structure: VIX futures curve remains in contango. VXK26 at 20.00 vs spot 18.29. Steepening front-month spread.

put_call_ratio.assessment: Equity PCR jumped from 0.46 to 0.54 Monday - still firmly in complacency zone (<0.70). Some hedging picked up on geopolitical news but not extreme. Index PCR at 1.05 shows institutional index hedging more active.

aaii_sentiment.historical_context: Bullish above 37.5% historical average. Bearish slightly elevated. Spread +11.6% is moderately bullish but not euphoric.
aaii_sentiment.contrarian_reading: Neutral-bullish. Retail not yet at extreme greed levels. The +11.6 spread leaves room before contrarian sell signals trigger (>20%).
aaii_sentiment.note: Next survey update due Thursday May 7. Current reading reflects pre-May Day holiday sentiment.

naaim_exposure.historical_context: NAAIM exposure at 94.15 is near historical highs. Active managers are almost fully invested - a classic late-cycle warning flag.
naaim_exposure.contrarian_reading: Bearish warning. Managers at 94%+ exposure have historically preceded corrections. This is one of the most reliable contrarian sell signals in the dataset.

smart_money_dumb_money.divergence: Widening. Smart money has been reducing risk into record highs while retail flows remain strong.
smart_money_dumb_money.assessment: Classic late-cycle divergence. Retail buying euphoria vs institutional caution. Dumb money confidence elevated on AI earnings momentum (Apple, Magnificent Seven).

margin_debt.assessment: Margin debt near record highs. Increased leverage amplifies downside risk. March data from NYSE due for April update.
margin_debt.contrarian_reading: Bearish warning. High margin debt + record prices = vulnerability to deleveraging cascade.

hyg_credit_spread.assessment: High yield spreads tightened 10bps to 2.85% - extremely tight by historical standards. Market pricing minimal credit risk despite geopolitical tensions.
hyg_credit_spread.contrarian_reading: Complacency signal. Sub-3% spreads with Middle East conflict ongoing suggests risk mispricing.

investor_advisors.assessment: Investment advisors remain bullish. 56% bulls vs 20% bears is a +36 spread - elevated but not yet at 2018/2021 extremes.
investor_advisors.contrarian_reading: Caution warranted above +40. Current +36 is yellow zone.

contrarian_indicators.overall: 3 of 5 primary contrarian flags flashing caution (NAAIM extreme, margin debt highs, tight credit spreads). AAII and VIX remain neutral.

contrarian_analysis.fear_greed_assessment: Fear & Greed at 66 is in upper Greed zone but not Extreme Greed. 5 of 7 components greedy. However, 'stock price strength' in Fear suggests market rally is narrow - fewer stocks participating in new highs. This internal dispersion is a warning. Contrarian stance: mild caution, not yet extreme.
contrarian_analysis.vix_assessment: VIX at 18.29 with premarket at ~17.65 is low. Realized vol (13.75) even lower. Market pricing calm despite Iran/UAE escalation. Contrarians note: low VIX + geopolitical risk = potential surprise catalyst. VIX under 20 with active conflict is unusual. Risk: complacency.
contrarian_analysis.aaii_assessment: AAII bull-bear spread at +11.6 is moderately bullish. Historical extremes are +25 to +30. Retail not yet at euphoria. Contrarian read: room for retail to get more bullish before exhaustion. Neutral-bullish for timing.
contrarian_analysis.naaim_assessment: CRITICAL CONTRARIAN SIGNAL. NAAIM at 94.15 is near all-time highs. Active managers are fully invested with minimal cash. This indicator has one of the best track records for predicting corrections when above 90. Historical precedent: 2007, 2018, 2021 peaks all saw NAAIM >90 before 10%+ drawdowns. Contrarian stance: bearish warning.

policy_cross_impact.trump_taco_summary: Trump announced 25% car/truck tariffs on EU last week, threatening escalation. Iran/UAE ceasefire under strain after UAE reported Iranian attacks - first since early April truce. Trump maintaining Hormuz blockade. Oil held above $103 (WTI), Brent >$111. Buffett inflation concerns circulating. Fed speakers this week: Williams (Mon), Bowman (Tue).
policy_cross_impact.policy_event_risk: High. Multiple live wires: (1) EU auto tariff escalation next week, (2) Iran/UAE conflict re-escalation risk, (3) Hormuz strait closure ongoing, (4) Fed policy uncertainty with services inflation sticky. Any one could trigger volatility spike.
policy_cross_impact.market_correlation: Market shrugging off geopolitical risk so far. S&P and Nasdaq at/near records despite Hormuz blockade. This disconnect is either resilience or complacency. Historically, markets ignore geopolitical risk until they don't.
policy_cross_impact.hedge_recommendation: Maintain tail risk hedges. VIX cheap at 18. Put spreads on SPY/QQQ cost-effective. Consider VIX calls or SPY put spreads for portfolios >70% equity.

tomorrow_watch: NAAIM survey release (Wednesday May 7) - critical contrarian update
AAII sentiment survey (Thursday May 8) - post-holiday retail read
Fed Governor Bowman speaks Tuesday 10:00 ET - watch for hawkish signals
New Home Sales + ISM Services (Tuesday) - services inflation focus
Iran/UAE ceasefire developments - any escalation could break complacency
VIX futures roll (Wednesday) - watch for term structure shifts
Earnings: ~80% of S&P have beaten EPS estimates this season - watch guidance revisions
